title: new undici Agent with connect.rejectUnauthorized: false — the HTTP client skips TLS certificate validation (MITM)

params.passDetail: The undici Agent validates server certificates (connect.rejectUnauthorized is not false) — TLS identity is verified for every request it dispatches.
params.failDetail: new undici Agent is created with connect: { rejectUnauthorized: false }, disabling TLS certificate validation for every request routed through it. When set as the global dispatcher (a common way to 'fix' cert errors for fetch/undici), the whole HTTP client silently trusts forged certificates and can be man-in-the-middled. Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; pass a trusted CA via connect.ca if a private CA is required.
params.absentCallDetail: No undici Agent construction in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: The undici Agent does not set connect.rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: connect: { rejectUnauthorized: false } on a shared undici Agent (often installed as the global dispatcher) disables MITM protection for the whole process's fetch/undici traffic. Pasted to silence a self-signed-cert error, it ships everywhere.
